title: indigofera procumbens
intro: Indigofera procumbens, also known as procumbent indigo, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to support liver and kidney function. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and promote overall well-being. Indigofera procumbens is often prepared as a herbal tea, powder, or extract, and is used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including jaundice, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
